title: Weingartia cardenasiana
taxonomy: Weingartia cardenasiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae, commonly known as cacti. It is native to Bolivia and is a relatively rare species. The plant is often grown for its unique, vase-shaped stems and vibrant flowers. Weingartia cardenasiana is a type of epiphytic cactus, meaning it grows on other plants or objects without deriving nutrients from them. As a member of the Cactaceae family, Weingartia cardenasiana is closely related to other cacti species.
